narrative.summary: You're stepping into Terroni's Yonge Street outpost where Southern Italian purists run the kitchen—the pizza arrives whole and unsliced because they want that thin crust crisp on your plate, not soggy from steam. The Tonnarelli alla Norcina comes slicked with truffle and crumbled sausage, and the Spaghetti Limone does that magic trick where three ingredients taste like summer on the Amalfi Coast. Check your water glass before you drink—apparently lipstick on the rim is part of the experience.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on a busy stretch of Yonge Street where the sidewalk hums with foot traffic. Inside, exposed brick walls meet dark wood tables under warm Edison bulb lighting—the kind of room that gets louder as the night goes on. Your water glass arrives (check it for lipstick, apparently) and you're shoulder-to-shoulder with couples and groups who've clearly been here before.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: Southern Italian purists run this kitchen, so your pizza arrives whole and unsliced—they want that thin crust to stay crisp on your plate, not soggy from steam. The Tonnarelli alla Norcina comes slicked with truffle and crumbled sausage, while the Spaghetti Limone does that magic trick where three ingredients taste like summer on the Amalfi Coast. Expect to spend $20-30 for pasta, a bit more if you're adding the Funghi Assoluti to start.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "The menu focuses on traditional pasta and thin-crust pizza, which arrives unsliced to preserve the crust's texture. Signature Dishes: Popular items include the Tonnarelli alla Norcina (truffle and sausage pasta), Spaghetti Limone, and the Funghi Assoluti appetizer. Widely recognized for its strict commitment to authentic Southern Italian cuisine.
Definitely give it a try you won't leave disappointed, quality of food so far being served no hiccups on my visits, except for lipstick lips stamped right on the water glass, don't rush washing the cups please keep it hygienic."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "We ordered the chanterelle risotto — it was absolutely delicious!
Fresh homemade bread, baked right there — you can really taste the care.
The house-made ice cream was delightful, and the cocktails were amazing too.
Lovely atmosphere and friendly, attentive staff.
A wonderful place — everything we ordered was perfectly prepared. Highly recommend!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Last night we spent our third Valentine’s together, and for the first time we celebrated at Terroni. It was pretty busy because of Valentine’s Day, but the atmosphere was honestly so beautiful. Seeing so many couples in love around us just gave such a nice vibe.

Overall it was a really lovely experience. Our waiter was kind and super quick with everything, and overall a really nice guy. I just feel like he didn’t have the best suggestions when we asked for recommendations — not a big deal, just something to note.

The food was delicious, and for dessert we tried the tiramisu. I’m a huge tiramisu lover and I try it everywhere, and I can genuinely say this was one of the best tiramisus I’ve ever had!

Definitely a memorable Valentine’s for us 🤍"
